description: Post-run-002 fixes: timeout, incremental commits, progress tracking, context conservation, graceful degradation, error logging, parallel tasks, auto-compact

evals.description: Agent exceeds 10 min, should log timeout and continue to next task
evals.prompt: You are in Phase 4 executing an autonomous plan. You launched a background research agent 11 minutes ago and it still hasn't returned. What do you do?
assertions: Mentions logging the timeout to progress.md
Does NOT retry the same agent
Continues to the next task
References the 10-minute hard timeout limit
Treats the timed-out task as a documented gap, not a blocker

evals.description: After task completion, should commit and push immediately
evals.prompt: You just finished Task 3 of an autonomous run — you created two new files and modified one existing file. What are your next steps before starting Task 4?
assertions: Commits the changes immediately (not batched at end)
Pushes after committing
Stages specific files (not git add -A or git add .)
Uses a descriptive commit message with type prefix (feat:/fix:/docs:/wip:)
Updates progress.md with Task 3 results before moving on

evals.description: After task completion, should append structured entry to progress.md
evals.prompt: Task 5 just completed successfully — you created research/analysis.md and modified projects/config.json. What do you write to progress.md?
assertions: Includes task status (PASS/FAIL/PARTIAL)
Includes timestamp in EST
Lists files created or modified
Includes the commit hash from the task's commit
Entry is appended (not overwriting previous entries)

evals.description: Large document should be read with targeted offset/limit, not fully
evals.prompt: Task 7 requires analyzing a 2000-line research document at research/big-report.md to extract the 'Recommendations' section. How do you approach reading this file?
assertions: Does NOT read the entire 2000-line file at once
Reads first ~50 lines to understand structure
Uses offset/limit to target the specific section needed
Mentions context conservation as the reason
If delegating to a sub-agent, gives specific questions rather than the full document

evals.description: Staged file contains API key, should unstage and warn
evals.prompt: You're about to commit after Task 2. You run git diff --staged and notice one of the files contains the line: API_KEY=sk-proj-abc123def456. What do you do?
assertions: Does NOT commit the file with the secret
Unstages or removes the secret from the file
Logs a warning about the detected secret
Moves the secret to .env or removes it entirely
Proceeds with the commit only after the secret is removed

evals.description: Sub-agent fails, should log the gap and continue
evals.prompt: During Task 4 of an autonomous run, you launched a sub-agent to research competitor pricing. The agent returned an error: 'WebSearch permission denied.' What do you do?
assertions: Does NOT retry the same failing operation in a loop
Logs the failure and what information is missing
Continues to the next task or completes what's possible
Documents the gap for the completion report
Does NOT stop execution or ask the user

evals.description: Task fails, should produce structured error log with retry guidance
evals.prompt: Task 6 failed — you were trying to create a Python script that calls an external API, but the API returned 403 Forbidden on every attempt. How do you log this failure?
assertions: Records what was attempted (the specific task and action)
Records what failed (API returned 403)
Includes enough detail for manual retry
Logs this in progress.md with structured format
The completion report will include this in a Failed Tasks section

evals.description: Two independent tasks in plan should be launched in parallel
evals.prompt: You're reading plan.md and see: Task 3 depends on Task 2, but Task 4 and Task 5 have 'Depends on: none' and are both ready. How do you execute them?
assertions: Launches Task 4 and Task 5 in parallel (not sequentially)
Each parallel task gets its own progress.md entry
Applies the 10-minute timeout to each parallel agent
Waits for both to complete (or timeout) before moving to dependent tasks
Does NOT launch Task 3 until Task 2 is done

evals.description: Context is high after reading many files, should proactively compact
evals.prompt: You're midway through Task 8 of a 10-task autonomous run. You've read 15 files, launched 6 sub-agents, and the tool outputs have been very large. You notice responses are getting slower and context feels heavy. What do you do?
assertions: Writes a checkpoint to progress.md BEFORE any compaction
Proactively manages context rather than waiting for system-forced compression
After compression, re-reads plan.md and progress.md to restore state
The checkpoint includes current task status and remaining tasks
Continues execution after restoring context
